g.data-narration: Welcome to IoT Logical Design using Python. Learn how to implement state machines, event-driven systems, and data pipelines.
g.text: IoT Systems - Logical Design Using Python
State Machines • Event-Driven Architecture • Data Pipelines

g.data-narration: Logical design defines abstract components and data flow in IoT, independent of hardware. It maps how data moves from sensors to processing.
g.text: What is Logical Design in IoT?
Abstract representation of functional components and their interactions

g.data-narration: Python enables rapid prototyping, has readable syntax, rich libraries, and runs cross-platform for quick IoT system testing.
g.text: Python's Role in IoT Logical Design

g.data-narration: State machines model IoT systems with distinct states like red, green, yellow, transitioning based on events like timer expiration.
g.text: Model systems with discrete states that transition based on events

g.data-narration: Traffic lights follow a strict cycle: red to green to yellow to red. Each state has a defined duration in the cycle times dictionary.
Event-driven architecture allows IoT systems to respond to sensor readings and user inputs using publish-subscribe patterns.
g.text: Systems respond to events like sensor readings or user inputs using publish-subscribe

g.data-narration: Data pipelines process sensor readings through three stages: filters validate data, processors transform it, and outputs transmit results.
g.text: Process sensor data through Filter → Process → Output stages

g.data-narration: The pipeline class chains filters, processors, and outputs together, returning None if data fails validation at any filter.
The EventManager implements publish-subscribe, allowing components to register for events and receive notifications when events occur.
g.text: Decoupled communication - publishers and subscribers don't know about each other

g.data-narration: The Observer pattern notifies all attached observers when a subject's state changes, useful for sensor-to-display scenarios.
Safety properties ensure the system never enters invalid states. Liveness ensures all states are eventually visited in the system.
g.text: return all(s in history for s in states)

g.data-narration: Follow best practices: separate concerns, use clear state machines, handle errors, create modular components, and test thoroughly.
g.text: Best Practices for IoT Logical Design

g.data-narration: Unit testing validates logical components before hardware integration, ensuring state transitions work correctly.
Python provides powerful tools for IoT logical design through state machines, event-driven patterns, and data pipelines, enabling rapid prototyping.
g.text: Key Takeaways: Python enables rapid IoT prototyping through state machines, event-driven patterns, and modular data pipelines
